query_text: Show completed passes that move the ball forward and bypass at least five opposition outfield players.
description: Semantic expression for the first high-bypass pass family. It proves that the semantic compiler can lower a newer football capability stack without treating recipes as the language boundary.

scope.summary: Selected real matches, both halves, from the selected attacking-team perspective.

anchor.summary: Completed controlled pass receptions whose physical release and controlled reception can be aligned to tracking.

bind.summary: Passer, receiver, ball, attacking direction, and expected active opposition outfield players.

measure.summary: Measure forward progression and count opponents who move from goal-side of the ball at release to behind the ball at controlled reception.

match.summary: Require forward progression above the declared metre threshold and bypass count above the declared count threshold.

outcome.summary: Classify a moment as HIGH_BYPASS_COMPLETED_PASS when both quantitative predicates pass.

judge.summary: Use the experimental high-bypass pass definition: forward progression at least 8 metres and at least 5 bypassed opponents by default.

return.summary: Return release/reception frames, passer, receiver, progression, bypassed players, coverage status, uncertainty, and replay evidence.
